reply_content:
@npittswarof2 I think Daniel is wrestling with the fact that his mentor was a three-dimensional human being, not some godlike saint. 

The “we’re moving away from bonsais” comment seems to me to be him still struggling with the fact that Mr. Miyagi killed someone in the Sekai Taikai back in the day, and now he himself just saw someone else die in that tournament and was unable to stop it. 

I think we’re going to see Daniel realize that “the Miyagi-Do way” was not always the peaceful “no be there” philosophy it grew into, and that this is a sign that anyone—or perhaps even any dojo—can change. 

I think we’ll see Daniel gain a new kind of respect for Mr. Miyagi and who he was as a person. He will realize the humanity of Mr. Miyagi and how Mr. Miyagi’s wisdom, kindness, and restraint didn’t develop overnight, just like his karate skills didn’t either.
